rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: UTG ~16-18%, HJ ~21%, CO ~25-28%, BTN ~40-44%, SB raise-or-fold. Opens are SMALLER from EP in modern solver play because more players remain to 3-bet.
rows.CommonMistakes: Opening too wide from EP; using one big size from every seat; limping outside the SB.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: low-stakes opens are too loose & too big from EP. Exploit: tighten and let them pay you off postflop; isolate their limps. Coach by drilling position-by-position to 90%.
rows.AlternativeLines: Limp-or-raise SB mix; flat ~2.25bb from all seats as a simplification.
rows.Notes: Production-Ready baseline.
rows.GTOBaseline: UTG ~16-18%, HJ ~21%, CO ~25-28%, BTN ~40-44%, SB raise-or-fold. Opens are SMALLER from EP in modern solver play because more players remain to 3-bet
rows.PopulationTendency: low-stakes opens are too loose & too big from EP
rows.ExploitAdjustment: tighten and let them pay you off postflop; isolate their limps
rows.WhyItHappens: Players lean on static memorized charts and ignore position, stack depth, and how many players are left to act.
rows.CoachingScript: When a player struggles with rfi by position: anchor on the baseline — Open tighter early, wider late. Then show the population read ('low-stakes opens are too loose & too big from EP'), assign one drill spot and one quiz, and review the common mistake next session.
rows.IntermediateExplanation: Open tighter early, wider late. Baseline: UTG ~16-18%, HJ ~21%, CO ~25-28%, BTN ~40-44%, SB raise-or-fold. Opens are SMALLER from EP in modern solver play because more players remain to 3-bet
rows.AdvancedExplanation: GTO baseline: UTG ~16-18%, HJ ~21%, CO ~25-28%, BTN ~40-44%, SB raise-or-fold. Opens are SMALLER from EP in modern solver play because more players remain to 3-bet. Exploit: tighten and let them pay you off postflop; isolate their limps

rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: ~2bb EP, ~2.3bb CO, ~2.5bb BTN, 3bb SB online; live 3-4bb due to more callers.
rows.CommonMistakes: Over-sizing opens online; under-sizing live where many players call.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: live players call anything, so size up for value; online regs punish large opens with 3-bets. Coach to match size to fold equity & rake.
rows.AlternativeLines: Geometric vs fixed sizing; larger SB to charge the BB.
rows.Notes: Modern: smaller from EP, not larger.
rows.GTOBaseline: ~2bb EP, ~2.3bb CO, ~2.5bb BTN, 3bb SB online; live 3-4bb due to more callers
rows.PopulationTendency: live players call anything, so size up for value; online regs punish large opens with 3-bets
rows.ExploitAdjustment: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency.
rows.WhyItHappens: Players lean on static memorized charts and ignore position, stack depth, and how many players are left to act.
rows.CoachingScript: When a player struggles with open-raise sizing: anchor on the baseline — ~2-2.5bb online, 3bb+ live. Then show the population read ('live players call anything, so size up for value; online regs punish large opens...'), assign one drill spot and one quiz, and review the common mistake next session.
rows.IntermediateExplanation: ~2-2.5bb online, 3bb+ live. Baseline: ~2bb EP, ~2.3bb CO, ~2.5bb BTN, 3bb SB online; live 3-4bb due to more callers
rows.AdvancedExplanation: GTO baseline: ~2bb EP, ~2.3bb CO, ~2.5bb BTN, 3bb SB online; live 3-4bb due to more callers. Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency.

rows.ShortExplanation: BB defends widest because it closes action.
rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: vs 2.5bb BTN ~44% defend; vs 3bb ~33%; BvB 50%+. Flat = suited/connected, 3-bet = polar. OOP realization is poor so defense is below MDF.
rows.CommonMistakes: Defending too wide with unplayable offsuit hands; never 3-betting; over-folding vs small opens.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: micro BBs over-fold flop to c-bets and 3-bet too little. Exploit: c-bet/barrel more, value thinner; 3-bet them lighter. Coach pot-odds + realization.
rows.AlternativeLines: Pure-call vs 3-bet-or-fold construction by hand class.
rows.Notes: Store defense as ranges per open-size. | governance: SolverVerified was 'Partial' (nuance tracked in VerificationTier/Pack_Manifests)

rows.PopulationTendency: micro BBs over-fold flop to c-bets and 3-bet too little
rows.ExploitAdjustment: c-bet/barrel more, value thinner; 3-bet them lighter
rows.WhyItHappens: Players lean on static memorized charts and ignore position, stack depth, and how many players are left to act.
rows.CoachingScript: When a player struggles with bb defense: anchor on the baseline — BB defends widest because it closes action. Then show the population read ('micro BBs over-fold flop to c-bets and 3-bet too little'), assign one drill spot and one quiz, and review the common mistake next session.
rows.BeginnerExplanation: BB defends widest because it closes action.

rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: 3-bet smaller IP (~3x), larger OOP (~4x). OOP/vs-wide = linear; IP/vs-tight = polar. 3-bet-or-fold from most seats; BTN may flat vs UTG.
rows.CommonMistakes: Flatting too much OOP; one-size 3-betting; no bluff 3-bets (face-up value).
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: pools 3-bet too tight & don't 4-bet bluff, so they over-fold to your 3-bets. Exploit: widen value 3-bets + add blocker bluffs. Coach linear-vs-polar by spot.
rows.AlternativeLines: Flat-call IP deep vs EP; merged 3-bets vs limpy pools.
rows.Notes: Sizing Production-Ready.
rows.GTOBaseline: 3-bet smaller IP (~3x), larger OOP (~4x). OOP/vs-wide = linear; IP/vs-tight = polar. 3-bet-or-fold from most seats; BTN may flat vs UTG
rows.PopulationTendency: pools 3-bet too tight & don't 4-bet bluff, so they over-fold to your 3-bets
rows.ExploitAdjustment: widen value 3-bets + add blocker bluffs
rows.WhyItHappens: Players lean on static memorized charts and ignore position, stack depth, and how many players are left to act.
rows.CoachingScript: When a player struggles with 3-bet sizing & shape: anchor on the baseline — IP ~3-3.5x, OOP ~4x; shape by matchup. Then show the population read ('pools 3-bet too tight & don't 4-bet bluff, so they over-fold to your 3-bets'), assign one drill spot and one quiz, and review the common mistake next session.
rows.IntermediateExplanation: IP ~3-3.5x, OOP ~4x; shape by matchup. Baseline: 3-bet smaller IP (~3x), larger OOP (~4x). OOP/vs-wide = linear; IP/vs-tight = polar. 3-bet-or-fold from most seats; BTN may flat vs UTG
rows.AdvancedExplanation: GTO baseline: 3-bet smaller IP (~3x), larger OOP (~4x). OOP/vs-wide = linear; IP/vs-tight = polar. 3-bet-or-fold from most seats; BTN may flat vs UTG. Exploit: widen value 3-bets + add blocker bluffs

rows.ShortExplanation: Fold most; call a continuing range; 4-bet polar.
rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline (UTG vs BTN): call ~33.6%, 4-bet ~12.4%, fold >50%. IP defends wider; OOP is more 4-bet-or-fold. AK/QQ often flat vs opens.
rows.CommonMistakes: Calling 3-bets too wide OOP; never 4-bet bluffing; stacking off 100bb with one pair.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: pools under-4-bet-bluff, so fold your 4-bets to their 5-bet jams unless KK+. Exploit: 4-bet-bluff more vs nitty 3-bettors; flat more vs maniacs. Coach SPR & commitment.
rows.AlternativeLines: Flat-heavy vs polar 4-bet trees by stake.
rows.Notes: Exact %s Needs-Verification. | governance: SolverVerified was 'Partial' (nuance tracked in VerificationTier/Pack_Manifests)
rows.GTOBaseline: GTO baseline (UTG vs BTN): call ~33.
rows.PopulationTendency: pools under-4-bet-bluff, so fold your 4-bets to their 5-bet jams unless KK+
rows.ExploitAdjustment: 4-bet-bluff more vs nitty 3-bettors; flat more vs maniacs
rows.WhyItHappens: Players lean on static memorized charts and ignore position, stack depth, and how many players are left to act.
rows.CoachingScript: When a player struggles with facing a 3-bet: anchor on the baseline — Fold most; call a continuing range; 4-bet polar. Then show the population read ('pools under-4-bet-bluff, so fold your 4-bets to their 5-bet jams unless KK+'), assign one drill spot and one quiz, and review the common mistake next session.

rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: 4-bet ~2.2-2.3x IP / 2.5-3x OOP the 3-bet, value QQ+/AK with A5s-A2s/blocker bluffs; ~2-3% EP widening to ~4-6% late.
rows.CommonMistakes: 4-bet bluffing without blockers; 4-bet/folding value; sizing too large and committing.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: stations don't fold to 4-bets, so 4-bet for value only vs them; nits over-fold, so bluff them. Coach blocker selection.
rows.AlternativeLines: Linear merged 4-bets vs very loose pools.
rows.Notes: Value:bluff ratios Needs-Verification. | governance: SolverVerified was 'Partial' (nuance tracked in VerificationTier/Pack_Manifests)

rows.CoachingScript: When a player struggles with 4-bet construction: anchor on the baseline — Polar: QQ+/AK + blocker bluffs. Then show the population read ('stations don't fold to 4-bets, so 4-bet for value only vs them; nits over-fold, ...'), assign one drill spot and one quiz, and review the common mistake next session.

rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: 5-bet = all-in at 100bb. KK+/AK jam pure; QQ/JJ at partial frequency (tighter vs EP). Light A5s 5-bet bluff is a high-stakes refinement.
rows.CommonMistakes: 5-bet bluffing at low stakes where nobody folds; folding KK to a 5-bet.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: 5-bet jams are massively under-bluffed at low stakes → fold everything but KK+ to a 5-bet. Coach to value-jam and skip bluffs.
rows.Notes: Most games: value-only 5-bets. | governance: SolverVerified was 'Partial' (nuance tracked in VerificationTier/Pack_Manifests)

rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: squeeze tighter for larger sizes; under ICM squeeze more for smaller sizes. The cold-caller rarely has hands that dominate you, so their chips subsidize the squeeze.
rows.CommonMistakes: Squeezing too small (priced-in callers); squeezing trash with no blockers.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: pools cold-call too much and don't adjust → squeeze wider for value. Coach blockers + sizing.
rows.AlternativeLines: Flat-squeeze mix IP with strong hands.
rows.Notes: Tune per pool/ICM. | governance: SolverVerified was 'Partial' (nuance tracked in VerificationTier/Pack_Manifests)

rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: with range+nut advantage bet small & often; without it, check more. Nut advantage (more nutted combos) enables polarization/overbets.
rows.CommonMistakes: Auto-c-betting every board; using one size; betting big without nut advantage.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: pools over-fold flop yet over-call rivers. Exploit: c-bet/barrel more, value thinner, bluff rivers less. Coach board-favored reads.
rows.Notes: Texture heuristics, verify nodes. | governance: SolverVerified was 'Partial' (nuance tracked in VerificationTier/Pack_Manifests)
rows.GTOBaseline: with range+nut advantage bet small & often; without it, check more. Nut advantage (more nutted combos) enables polarization/overbets
rows.PopulationTendency: pools over-fold flop yet over-call rivers
rows.ExploitAdjustment: c-bet/barrel more, value thinner, bluff rivers less
rows.WhyItHappens: Players judge hands by absolute strength and auto-pilot, instead of reading board texture and whose range the board favors.
rows.CoachingScript: When a player struggles with range vs nut advantage: anchor on the baseline — Who's favored decides sizing. Then show the population read ('pools over-fold flop yet over-call rivers'), assign one drill spot and one quiz, and review the common mistake next session.

rows.ShortExplanation: Small on range boards, polar on static.
rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: ~25-33% pot on range-advantage boards at high frequency; 66%+/overbet on static nut-advantage boards.
rows.CommonMistakes: One-size-fits-all c-bets; big-betting medium hands.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: small c-bets print vs over-folders. Exploit: downsize & increase frequency on dry boards. Coach size-to-range.
rows.Notes: — | governance: SolverVerified was 'Partial' (nuance tracked in VerificationTier/Pack_Manifests)

rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: A/K/Q-high & paired boards favor the aggressor (range bet); low/connected favor the caller (check more). Dynamic boards → bet bigger for protection.
rows.CommonMistakes: Treating all boards the same; barreling caller-favored boards.
rows.CoachingNotes: Coach: drill 40 flops labeling favored range. Exploit: attack capped ranges on aggressor-favored turns.
rows.Notes: — | governance: SolverVerified was 'Partial' (nuance tracked in VerificationTier/Pack_Manifests)

rows.ShortExplanation: Barrel cards that favor you + carry blockers.
rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: barrel turns/rivers that improve your range or scare the caller; choose bluffs with equity/blockers that can keep firing.
rows.CommonMistakes: Auto-barreling every card; giving up the instant called; bluffing without blockers.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: over-folders fold turns → barrel more; stations don't → value only. Coach card-by-card re-evaluation.
rows.Notes: — | governance: SolverVerified was 'Partial' (nuance tracked in VerificationTier/Pack_Manifests)

rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: check-raise sets/two-pair/strong pairs + draws/blockers on boards where villain over-c-bets. Defends OOP ranges vs high-frequency c-bets.
rows.CommonMistakes: Check-raising too thin; no bluffs (face-up); never check-raising (exploitable).
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: pools under-bluff check-raises and over-fold to them → bluff-raise more, fold to their raises. Coach board selection.
rows.Notes: — | governance: SolverVerified was 'Partial' (nuance tracked in VerificationTier/Pack_Manifests)

rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: float IP with equity/blockers and a turn plan; probe (lead) turns after the aggressor checks back (capped range).
rows.CommonMistakes: Floating pure air; probing into uncapped checks.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: regs barrel too much → float wider; passive players check back capped → probe relentlessly. Coach capped-range logic.
rows.AlternativeLines: IP raise vs float by board.
rows.Notes: — | governance: SolverVerified was 'Partial' (nuance tracked in VerificationTier/Pack_Manifests)

rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: MDF = pot/(pot+bet) — pot bet ~50%, half-pot ~67%, 2x overbet ~33%. Call hands that unblock bluffs and beat the value:bluff ratio.
rows.CommonMistakes: Calling by absolute hand strength; over-folding vs aggression; over-calling vs under-bluffers.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: vs under-bluffing pools, fold more to big river bets; vs over-bluffers, call light. CONTESTED which pools do which — verify.
rows.Notes: River tendency contested. | governance: SolverVerified was 'Partial' (nuance tracked in VerificationTier/Pack_Manifests)

rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: bet thin vs stations & capped ranges; size to be called by worse. Opponent-dependent.
rows.CommonMistakes: Checking back clear value; value-betting into ranges that only continue with better.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: stations pay thin value → widen value, cut bluffs; nits don't → check more. Coach 'who calls worse?'

rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: overbet static, nut-advantaged run-outs with nutted value + blocker bluffs to pressure the whole range.
rows.CommonMistakes: Overbetting without nut advantage; on dynamic boards; with medium hands.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: overbets are under-bluffed at low stakes → respect them; use them yourself vs capped ranges. Coach nut-advantage check.

rows.ShortExplanation: Small OOP bets to set the price.
rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: small blocking bets with thin value/showdown hands to get value cheaply and deny a big raise.
rows.CommonMistakes: Block-betting too thin (turning showdown value into a bluff-catch); vs aggressive raisers.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: block bets work vs passive players who just call; aggressive players raise them → check-call instead. Coach price control.

rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: compute SPR on the flop; low SPR → commitment-oriented (stack off wider), high SPR → more maneuvering and fewer stack-offs.
rows.CommonMistakes: Stacking off wrong hands; no preflop SPR plan; over/under-committing.
rows.CoachingNotes: Coach: map commitment thresholds by SPR before betting. Exploit: set SPR to trap stations or pressure nits.

rows.ShortExplanation: Be the shover, not the caller.
rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: 5bb pure jam-or-fold; 10bb jam UTG ~12-13% → BTN ~38% → SB ~67-75%. Jamming ranges are wider than call-off ranges.
rows.CommonMistakes: Open-folding spots that should jam; min-raise/folding short; over-jamming weak offsuit aces from EP.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: opponents call jams too wide and reshove too little → jam for fold equity, value-jam vs the callers. Coach charts to 90%.
rows.Notes: chipEV Production-Ready.

rows.ShortExplanation: Re-jam over a steal with fold equity.
rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: vs a 20bb BTN open the BB can reshove KK-22, AK-AJ, ATo-A9o, A4o-A2o + mixed suited; antes widen it. Core 6-15bb skill.
rows.CommonMistakes: Flatting OOP short (no fold equity); reshoving too tight; ignoring antes.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: pools under-reshove → resteal wider; they over-fold to reshoves. Coach depth/position reshove ranges.
rows.AlternativeLines: Flat-call IP with a plan when deeper.
rows.Notes: — | governance: SolverVerified was 'Partial' (nuance tracked in VerificationTier/Pack_Manifests)

rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: call-off ranges are tighter than jamming ranges and depend on the shover's position (wider vs SB, tighter vs UTG) and antes.
rows.CommonMistakes: Calling jams with dominated hands; over-folding clear calls.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: opponents jam too wide late → widen call-offs vs SB/BTN jams. Coach 'jam wide, call tight'.

rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: 30-40bb mix flat vs 3-bet; 20-25bb a raise/3-bet-jam-or-fold game; low SPR postflop. Antes widen everything.
rows.CommonMistakes: Playing 20bb like 100bb; flatting 3-bets at low SPR; deep-postflop habits at 25bb.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: pools over-flat OOP and under-3-bet-jam → resteal wider. Coach the identity of each band.

rows.WhyItHappens: Players keep deep-stack ranges as stacks shrink and under-use the reshove, because antes and low SPR feel unfamiliar.

rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: antes raise the reward of stealing → open/steal/jam wider (~+20-30% hands with full antes).
rows.CommonMistakes: Keeping no-ante ranges once antes appear.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: many players don't widen with antes → out-steal them. Coach ante-adjusted ranges.

rows.ShortExplanation: Need more equity to stack off near pay jumps.
rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: required equity rises from ~33% (chipEV) to ~37-38% (normal bubble) up to ~61% (high bubble factor). ICM is present from hand 1 (~1.8% RP in a 200-runner).
rows.CommonMistakes: Calling off chipEV-wide on bubbles; ignoring pay jumps; flatting when you should fold.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: recreationals ignore ICM and call too wide → don't bluff them off, let them spew; pressure ICM-aware mid-stacks. Coach risk-premium math.
rows.Notes: Exact %s engine-specific. | governance: SolverVerified was 'Partial' (nuance tracked in VerificationTier/Pack_Manifests)

rows.ShortExplanation: Ratio of $ lost busting to $ gained winning.
rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: mid-stacks carry the highest bubble factor (most to lose); big stacks lowest (can pressure); short stacks feel it LESS than mids.
rows.CommonMistakes: Treating every opponent with one bubble factor; big stack playing scared.
rows.CoachingNotes: Coach per-opponent bubble factor; exploit by pressuring covered mids and avoiding the covering stack.

rows.ShortExplanation: Tighten, shift to blockers, shrink sizings.
rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: ranges ~1.4%+ tighter and Ax-blocker-shifted; QQ flats more; 3-bets compress (TT/99 flat); c-bet ~20% pot at ~91% frequency on the bubble.
rows.CommonMistakes: Using chipEV ranges under ICM; over-3-betting for value on bubbles; large sizings.
rows.CoachingNotes: Coach the shift from chipEV to ICM ranges; exploit ICM-unaware opponents who don't tighten.

rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: the big stack's low bubble factor lets it pressure mediums who can't risk busting — the single biggest ICM edge.
rows.CommonMistakes: Sitting passively with a big stack; tangling with the other big stack.
rows.CoachingNotes: Coach: pressure covered mediums, avoid coin-flips vs the cover. Exploit ICM-scared mids.

rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: once locked for a seat, chips are near-worthless — fold huge equity (even AA) if shorter stacks will likely bust first.
rows.CommonMistakes: Accumulating above the lock; gambling for a chip lead; chipEV play in a step-payout.
rows.CoachingNotes: Coach the lock threshold; exploit opponents who gamble above the lock.

rows.LongExplanation: GTO baseline: bounties add immediate equity and can push bubble factor below 1 (negative risk premium; ~-4.7% RP on hand 1 of a 200-runner PKO) → call wider when you cover.
rows.CommonMistakes: Ignoring the bounty; calling tight when covering an all-in for a bounty.
rows.CoachingNotes: Population: many misprice bounties → widen covering call-offs. Coach bounty-adjusted ranges.

rows.CoachingNotes: Coach 'who calls worse?' before betting. Highest-EV adjustment at low stakes.

rows.CoachingNotes: Coach: attack their folds, believe their aggression.

rows.IntermediateExplanation: Count combos to read ranges. Baseline: Baseline: pair=6, suited=4, offsuit=12, any specific two-card hand=16.
rows.AdvancedExplanation: Baseline: pair=6, suited=4, offsuit=12, any specific two-card hand=16. Blockers remove combos (e.g., AA cuts villain's AK from 16 to 8). Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency.

rows.LongExplanation: Baseline: a flop check-back removes most strong hands → the turn range is capped/medium → attack with probes/barrels.
rows.CommonMistakes: Checking into uncapped ranges; not attacking capped ones.
rows.CoachingNotes: Coach capped-vs-uncapped recognition; exploit passive check-backs.
rows.Notes: — | governance: SolverVerified was 'Partial' (nuance tracked in VerificationTier/Pack_Manifests)
rows.GTOBaseline: Baseline: a flop check-back removes most strong hands → the turn range is capped/medium → attack with probes/barrels.
rows.PopulationTendency: See Population_Exploits for the matching pool read.
rows.ExploitAdjustment: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency.
rows.WhyItHappens: Players track their own hand but not the opponent's range, so they miss blockers and capped lines.
rows.CoachingScript: When a player struggles with capped ranges: anchor on the baseline — A check-back caps the range. Then show the population read ('See Population_Exploits for the matching pool read.'), assign one drill spot and one quiz, and review the common mistake next session.
rows.IntermediateExplanation: A check-back caps the range. Baseline: Baseline: a flop check-back removes most strong hands → the turn range is capped/medium → attack with probes/barrels.
rows.AdvancedExplanation: Baseline: a flop check-back removes most strong hands → the turn range is capped/medium → attack with probes/barrels. Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency.

rows.LongExplanation: Baseline: identify tilt triggers, set a stop-loss, quit-on-tilt rule, and a warm-up routine to protect A-game.
rows.CommonMistakes: Revenge-spewing a specific player; chasing losses; playing through tilt.
rows.CoachingNotes: Coach a tilt profile + stop-loss; exploit tilted opponents (value heavy, don't bluff).
rows.GTOBaseline: Baseline: identify tilt triggers, set a stop-loss, quit-on-tilt rule, and a warm-up routine to protect A-game.
rows.PopulationTendency: See Population_Exploits for the matching pool read.
rows.ExploitAdjustment: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency.
rows.WhyItHappens: Emotional and cognitive biases (loss-chasing, results-orientation, ego) override disciplined, EV-based decisions.
rows.CoachingScript: When a player struggles with tilt control: anchor on the baseline — Keep decisions EV-based, not emotional. Then show the population read ('See Population_Exploits for the matching pool read.'), assign one drill spot and one quiz, and review the common mistake next session.
rows.IntermediateExplanation: Keep decisions EV-based, not emotional. Baseline: Baseline: identify tilt triggers, set a stop-loss, quit-on-tilt rule, and a warm-up routine to protect A-game.
rows.AdvancedExplanation: Baseline: identify tilt triggers, set a stop-loss, quit-on-tilt rule, and a warm-up routine to protect A-game. Exploit: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency.

rows.ShortExplanation: Expect swings; judge process not results.
rows.LongExplanation: Baseline: even +EV play has long downswings; run variance sims for your win-rate/SD to set realistic expectations and a downswing protocol.
rows.CommonMistakes: Results-oriented thinking; moving up to recover; quitting after normal variance.
rows.CoachingNotes: Coach process goals + downswing action plan.
rows.GTOBaseline: Baseline: even +EV play has long downswings; run variance sims for your win-rate/SD to set realistic expectations and a downswing protocol.
rows.PopulationTendency: See Population_Exploits for the matching pool read.
rows.ExploitAdjustment: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency.
rows.WhyItHappens: Emotional and cognitive biases (loss-chasing, results-orientation, ego) override disciplined, EV-based decisions.
rows.CoachingScript: When a player struggles with variance & downswings: anchor on the baseline — Expect swings; judge process not results. Then show the population read ('See Population_Exploits for the matching pool read.'), assign one drill spot and one quiz, and review the common mistake next session.

rows.LongExplanation: Baseline: ~30-50+ buy-ins for cash, ~100+ for MTTs; move down in downswings; never play underrolled.
rows.CommonMistakes: Playing underrolled; not moving down; tilt deposits; over-withdrawing.
rows.CoachingNotes: Coach a bankroll plan + move-up/down thresholds; track results.
rows.GTOBaseline: Baseline: ~30-50+ buy-ins for cash, ~100+ for MTTs; move down in downswings; never play underrolled.
rows.PopulationTendency: See Population_Exploits for the matching pool read.
rows.ExploitAdjustment: Deviate from the baseline toward the pool's specific tendency.
rows.WhyItHappens: Players let ego and short-term results drive stakes, ignoring variance and bankroll math.
rows.CoachingScript: When a player struggles with bankroll requirements: anchor on the baseline — Roll sized to game & variance. Then show the population read ('See Population_Exploits for the matching pool read.'), assign one drill spot and one quiz, and review the common mistake next session.
